meta.title: FluxFiles — The file manager that embeds anywhere
meta.description: PHP 8.1+ file manager with S3, R2, and local storage. Alpine.js UI. Embed via iframe + JavaScript SDK.

hero.headline: The file manager that embeds anywhere
hero.sub: PHP ≥ 8.1 + Flysystem 3. Alpine.js UI. Local, S3, Cloudflare R2. Embed with a few lines of JavaScript.

demo.label: Live Preview
demo.headline: See it in action
demo.cap_desktop: Grid view with instant WebP thumbnails
demo.cap_activity: Activity log — kept in your own bucket
demo.cap_doctor: Bucket Doctor — S3/R2 health checks + fixes

features.label: Features
features.headline: Everything a modern file manager needs
features.sub: Built for developers who need flexibility.
features.storage_desc: Local, AWS S3, Cloudflare R2 via Flysystem v3 — swap with config, no code change.
features.embed_desc: iframe + postMessage SDK. Standalone UI is Alpine.js (zero build). Works with any framework.
features.jwt_desc: Per-user path prefix, permission claims, disk whitelist, owner-only mode.
features.seo_desc: Title, alt, caption, tags — S3 object metadata (cloud) or sidecar JSON (local). Full-text search.
features.presign_desc: Browser uploads directly to S3/R2 — zero bandwidth on your server.
features.i18n_desc: Default English. Set locale via SDK, URL (?locale=), or FLUXFILES_LOCALE. RTL for Arabic.
features.ai_desc: Claude / OpenAI vision — auto alt text, title, tags.
features.crop_desc: Inline crop tool with aspect ratio presets.
features.optimize_desc: Auto WebP variants — thumb, medium, large.
features.search_desc: SQLite FTS5 across names, titles, tags.
features.darkmode_desc: Light / dark / auto in UI and SDK.
features.byob_desc: Users bring their own S3/R2 — credentials encrypted in JWT (AES-256-GCM).
features.dup_desc: SHA-256 match skips redundant uploads unless you force overwrite.
features.tokenrefresh_desc: onTokenRefresh on 401 — coalesced refresh, retry, and updateToken() for proactive rotation.
features.audit_desc: All write actions logged for compliance.
features.ratelimit_desc: Token bucket per user for API protection.
features.quota_desc: Per-user storage limits via JWT claims.
features.doctor_desc: Diagnose any S3/R2 disk — credentials, read/write/delete, presign, CORS, multipart — with ready-to-paste IAM & CORS fixes. Onboard BYOB buckets in minutes.
features.node_desc: Mint FluxFiles tokens from any JS backend (Express, Next.js, Nuxt) with @fluxfiles/node — byte-compatible with the PHP core. No PHP required to issue tokens.

cta.headline: Ready to embed FluxFiles?
cta.sub: Add a production-ready file manager to your app in minutes — no framework lock-in, MIT licensed.

adapters.label: Integrations
adapters.headline: Drop-in adapters for your stack
adapters.sub: Official adapters embed FluxFiles natively in the frameworks and editors you already use.

embed.label: Embed
embed.headline: Embed it in a few lines
embed.sub: Load fluxfiles.js, pass a server-minted JWT, and drop a full file manager — or a file-picker modal — into any page.
embed.note: Tokens are minted server-side — never ship your secret to the browser.

faq.label: FAQ
faq.headline: Frequently asked questions
faq.oss_q: Is FluxFiles free and open source?
faq.oss_a: Yes — MIT licensed, free to self-host, and published on Composer and npm. No paid tiers, no usage limits.
faq.storage_a: Wherever you point it: local disk, AWS S3, or Cloudflare R2 via Flysystem. You own the storage — nothing goes through a third party.
faq.auth_a: Stateless JWT (HS256): you mint a token server-side with granular claims (permissions, disks, path scope, limits), so each user sees only what you allow.
faq.accounts_q: Do my users need a FluxFiles account?
faq.accounts_a: No. FluxFiles trusts the JWT from your own app, so it plugs into your existing auth — there's no separate user system.
faq.embed_q: Can I embed it in my existing app or editor?
faq.embed_a: Yes — the iframe + JS SDK works in any page, with official adapters for Laravel, WordPress, React, Vue/Nuxt, CKEditor 4 and TinyMCE.
faq.run_q: What do I need to run it?
faq.run_a: PHP 8.1+ with common extensions and Composer. Point it at a disk, set a secret and allowed origins, then serve — it runs on a small VPS.

byob.label: The moat · BYOB
byob.headline: Your customers' files stay in your customers' buckets
byob.sub: FluxFiles is the embeddable file manager where each tenant connects their own S3/R2 bucket — and you never store their data or their credentials.
byob.p1_desc: Files live in the customer's own cloud account and region — built for GDPR, compliance, and enterprise procurement.
byob.p2_title: Zero data, zero creds at rest
byob.p2_desc: Bucket credentials are AES-256-GCM encrypted inside the JWT and decrypted only at runtime — never written to a database or a log.
byob.p3_title: Storage cost that scales to zero
byob.p3_desc: Storage and egress are billed to each customer, not to you. No storage bill that grows with your user base.
byob.p4_desc: No central database. Drop the picker into any app by iframe or SDK; your backend mints a scoped, short-lived token.
byob.how: How it works: your app encrypts the tenant’s bucket credentials into a short-lived JWT (HKDF-SHA256 + AES-256-GCM). FluxFiles decrypts them only to run the requested operation.

saas.label: vs. file-API SaaS
saas.headline: Self-hosted and embeddable — not another upload SaaS
saas.sub: Hosted file APIs are great, but they store your data and bill per use. FluxFiles runs in your stack, with each tenant on their own bucket.
saas.note: Comparison reflects typical default offerings; each product excels in its own space. FluxFiles’ niche is the embeddable, self-hosted, bring-your-own-bucket combination.

who.label: Who it's for
who.headline: Built for teams that ship file features
who.sub: If your product needs uploads, FluxFiles drops in — without making you the landlord of your customers' data.
who.p1_desc: Give every customer a file manager on their own bucket. Per-tenant token scoping and data sovereignty are built in.
who.p2_desc: Ship a polished media library to each client without standing up storage infrastructure per project.
who.p3_desc: Drop a media picker into CKEditor, TinyMCE, or your own editor via the iframe SDK.
who.p4_desc: Add uploads, previews and audited file operations to admin panels in an afternoon.
